front: Sculpture: chryselephantine seated statue of Zeus at Olympia, enthroned holding a scepter; one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World
Sculpture: Pausanias says this work wore a glass robe and held a Nike figurine
Sculpture: Napoleon's pose in Ingres's Napoleon I on His Imperial Throne was modeled on descriptions of this statue
Sculpture: Phidias was apocryphally murdered after completing it; it was repeatedly rubbed with olive oil due to the marsh
Sculpture: chryselephantine Athena in the naos of the Parthenon; ~41 feet tall; holds a Nike
Sculpture: Phidias himself (as a bald old man throwing a stone) and Pericles appear on the circular shield alongside an Amazonomachy
Sculpture: triple-crested helmet with sphinx flanked by two pegasi; coiled snake between the shield and feet; Aegis with snake tassels
Sculpture: Pericles' enemies accused Phidias of embezzling gold meant for this statue
Sculpture: supervised the creation of the Elgin Marbles, including the Panathenaic frieze; Lawrence Alma-Tadema depicted him presenting the frieze to his friends
Sculptor: Mark Barr used the Greek letter phi — chosen to honor this sculptor's name — as the symbol for the golden ratio
Sculptor: created the Athena Promachos — a giant bronze statue of Athena on the Acropolis
Sculptor: came second to Polykleitos in the contest to sculpt a wounded Amazon for the Temple of Artemis
Sculptor: an inscription calling a wrestler beautiful appears on the little finger of one of his statues
